summary: Government assistance through programs like Medicaid and CHIP significantly increases eye care service utilization among low-income individuals by reducing financial barriers and improving access to providers. Evidence consistently links these interventions with improved outcomes in marginalized communities facing prior exclusions due to cost or geographic isolation.
